SEARCH
Package dev.jeka.core.api.tooling.git

Class JkGit

java.lang.Object
dev.jeka.core.api.system.JkAbstractProcess<JkGit>
dev.jeka.core.api.tooling.git.JkGit
All Implemented Interfaces:
Runnable
public final class JkGit extends JkAbstractProcess<JkGit>
A utility class for executing Git commands within a specific directory. This class extends JkAbstractProcess to facilitate the execution of process commands.

      public static JkGit of(Path dir)
      Creates a new JkGit instance pointing on the specified working directory.

      Creates a new JkGit instance pointing on the specified working directory.

      public static JkGit of()

      Returns the current branch name.

      public String getRemoteUrl()
      Retrieves the URL of the "origin" remote for the Git repository.
      Returns:
      The URL of the "origin" remote as a string.

    • isSyncWithRemote

      public boolean isSyncWithRemote()
      Checks if the local branch is in sync with the remote branch.

    • isWorkspaceDirty

      public boolean isWorkspaceDirty()
      Checks if the workspace is dirty by executing the 'git diff HEAD --stat' command and checking if the output is empty.
      Returns:
      true if the workspace is dirty, false otherwise.

    • getCurrentCommit

      public String getCurrentCommit()
      Returns the current commit of the Git repository.

    • getTagsOnCurrentCommit

      public List<String> getTagsOnCurrentCommit()
      Returns a list of tags associated with the current commit.

    • getLastCommitMessageMultiLine

      public List<String> getLastCommitMessageMultiLine()
      Returns the last commit message as a list of strings. Each line of the message is represented as a separate element in the list.

    • getLastCommitMessage

      public String getLastCommitMessage()
      Returns the last commit message.

    • extractSuffixFromLastCommitMessage

      public String extractSuffixFromLastCommitMessage(String prefix)
      Extracts information from the last commit message title.

      This method splits the commit message in separated words, then looks for the fist word starting with the specified prefix. The returned suffix is the word found minus the prefix.

      For example, if the title is 'Release:0.9.5.RC1 : Rework Dependencies', then invoking this method with 'Release:' argument will return '0.9.5.RC1'.

      This method returns null if no such prefix found.

    • tagAndPush

      public JkGit tagAndPush(String name)
      Puts a tag at the HEAD of the current branch and pushes it to the remote repository.

    • tag

      public JkGit tag(String tagName)
      Adds a tag at the HEAD of the current branch.

    • getVersionFromCommitMessage

      public String getVersionFromCommitMessage(String prefixKeyword)
      Returns version according the last commit message.

      If the commit message contains a word starting with the specified prefix keyword then the substring following this suffix will be returned.

      If no such prefix found, then a version formatted as [branch]-SNAPSHOT will be returned

    • getVersionFromTag

      public String getVersionFromTag(String prefix)
      Returns version according the tags found on current commit.

      If the current commit has a tag starting by prefix and the workspace is not dirty, then the version is this tag name on the current commit.

      If there is many tags starting with prefix on the current commit, only the last one in alphabetical order is taken in account.

      If no tag starting with prefix is present on the current commit or the workspace is dirty, the version will value [branch]-SNAPSHOT.

      If prefix is empty, any tag will match.

    • getVersionFromTag

      public String getVersionFromTag()
      Returns version according the tags found on current commit.
      See Also:
      • getVersionFromTag(String)

    • getJkVersionFromTag

      public JkVersion getJkVersionFromTag()
      Returns the JkVersion extracted from the tags of the current commit.
      See Also:
      • getVersionFromTag(String)

    • getCommitMessagesSinceLastTag

      public List<String> getCommitMessagesSinceLastTag()
      Returns the distinct last commit messages since last tag in the current branch.

    • getLatestTag

      public String getLatestTag()
      Returns the latest tag in the Git repository.

    • tagRemote

      public void tagRemote()
      Tags the remote repository with a new tag.

      This method performs the following steps:

      • Prints the existing tags on the origin.
      • Checks if the Git workspace is dirty. If it is, it prints an error message and returns.
      • Checks if the current tracking branch is aligned with the remote branch. If it is not, it prints an error message and returns.
      • Prompts the user to enter a new tag.
      • Tags the current commit with the new tag and pushes the tag to the remote repository.
      Note: It is necessary to have a clean Git workspace and a tracking branch aligned with the remote branch in order to tag the remote repository.

    • diiff

      public JkGit.FileList diiff(String commitFrom, String commitTo)
      Executes a Git diff command between the specified commits and returns a list of files changed.
      Parameters:
      commitFrom - The starting commit of the diff.
      commitTo - The ending commit of the diff. This can value HEAD.
      Returns:
      A FileList instance containing the list of files changed between the two commits.

    • getRemoteTagCommit

      public String getRemoteTagCommit(String repoUrl, String tag)
      Retrieves the commit hash associated with a specified tag from a remote Git repository. If the tag is blank, the HEAD reference is used instead.
      Parameters:
      tag - The tag name whose associated commit is to be fetched. If blank, the HEAD reference will be used.
      Returns:
      The commit hash associated with the given tag, or null if no commit is found.

    • getRemoteTagAsStrings

      public List<String> getRemoteTagAsStrings(String repoUrl)
      Retrieves a list of tags from a remote Git repository.

    • getRemoteTags

      public List<JkGit.Tag> getRemoteTags(String repoUrl)
      Retrieves a list of tags from a remote Git repository.
      Parameters:
      repoUrl - The URL of the remote Git repository to fetch tags from.
      Returns:
      A list of Tag objects representing the tags available in the remote Git repository.

    • getRemoteDefaultBranch

      public String getRemoteDefaultBranch(String repoUrl)
      Retrieves the default branch of a remote Git repository specified by its URL. This method uses the 'git ls-remote --symref' command to identify the default branch. If no default branch is found, or the repository URL is invalid, this method returns null.

    • copy

      public JkGit copy()
      Overrides:
      copy in class JkAbstractProcess<JkGit>
